## Step 4: Merge and inspect history

The team approves your proposal, so it is time to integrate it.

### 📖 Theory: Merging updates main history

Merging combines work from one branch into another and updates project history.

- After merge, branch changes become part of main.
- Commit history helps audit and understand project evolution.
- Cleaning up merged branches keeps repositories easier to navigate.

Read more:

- [Merging a pull request](https://docs.github.com/en/pull-requests/collaborating-with-pull-requests/incorporating-changes-from-a-pull-request/merging-a-pull-request)
- [Viewing commit history](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/working-with-files/using-files/viewing-and-understanding-files#viewing-the-commit-history-of-a-repository)

### ⌨️ Activity: Merge your pull request and review the timeline

1. Merge your open pull request into main.
1. (optional) Delete the branch `feature/onboarding-rules` after merge to keep branches tidy.
1. Open the repository commit history and find the merge-related entry.
1. Confirm that `handbook.md` on main now includes the onboarding checklist.

<details>
<summary>Having trouble? 🤷</summary><br/>

- If you cannot merge, make sure the pull request is open and there are no unresolved conflicts.
- If the delete branch option is missing, manually delete it from the branch selector.

</details>
